---
applyTo: "{packages/web-platform/web-core/**,packages/web-platform/web-core-e2e/**,packages/react/**/element-template/**,packages/webpack/react-webpack-plugin/**,packages/rspeedy/lynx-bundle-rslib-config/**}"
---

ReactLynx element templates are compile-time assets collected by the React webpack plugin and passed to web-core as `encodeData.elementTemplates`. The web binary encoder may normalize a collected template record into the persisted asset array, but the decoded `ElementTemplates` section should use only that array shape. For web-core CSR support, emit/register the binary `ElementTemplates` section before `LepusCode`, and include builtins such as `_et_builtin_raw_text` in the asset array rather than synthesizing them in Rust. Avoid Rust repeatedly reflecting over the compiled-template JS object, deserializing JS-built metadata blobs, or using per-node `js -> rust -> browser DOM` calls while registering definitions: TypeScript should traverse decoded assets once, build the inert HTML `<template>` DOM directly, and update Rust metadata incrementally through an `ElementTemplateDefinition` Rust object whose add/update methods take only numbers, strings, booleans, null markers, or undefined. Keep those definition-construction methods on `ElementTemplateDefinition`; `MainThreadWasmContext` should only receive the finished definition object for registration. Keep template DOM, slot anchors, clone, insert, remove, and serialized JS object construction in TypeScript; Rust/WASM keeps template metadata, unique-id registration, dynamic class/style updates, CSSOG refreshes, event registration, and slot ownership state. Use invisible anchors for element slots so normal child traversal does not include placeholder nodes.
For client inline style and element-template style handling, keep `transformVW`/`transformVH`/`transformREM` as `MainThreadWasmContext` state after construction; do not re-pass those transform flags through per-call style APIs or duplicate them on element-template builders.
For web-core client unique IDs, TS-created elements, including element-template clones, should set the JS `uniqueIdSymbol` in TypeScript after Rust returns the id. Keep `__GetElementUniqueID` as the single reader that checks symbol/property/attribute forms.
When building template definitions through custom elements, setting `text` on `x-text` or `raw-text` can run web-elements' `RawTextAttributes` handler and append a text node before the HTML template is cloned. Clear that construction-time side effect in the template definition; the cloned runtime instance should be the only place where the `text` attribute materializes text content.
